name: Integer Coercion Error
description: Integer coercion refers to flaws in type casting, extension, or truncation of primitive data types. When a value is converted between integer types of different sizes or signedness, data can be lost or misinterpreted, potentially leading to buffer overflows or other memory corruption.
detection_pattern: Look for INGRESS or TRANSFORM nodes where an integer value undergoes type casting (e.g., long to int, signed to unsigned) before being used at a STORAGE or STRUCTURAL node (such as buffer size calculation) without a CONTROL node that validates the value is within the target type's range. The missing mediator is range validation before type conversion.
